danger_signals.visual_form: Contrasting-colored bullets and energy beams fired toward player craft
Multiple Crystal faction units appearing on-screen in formation or chaotic patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating danger; density indicates stage difficulty progression

danger_signals.visual_form: Large, distinct alien mechanical entity with Crystal aesthetic, often screen-filling
danger_signals.meaning: Stage climax; high-damage attack pattern incoming

danger_signals.visual_form: Background changes, increased particle effects, faster enemy spawn rates
danger_signals.meaning: World state shift; difficulty ramping across 8-stage loop

danger_signals.visual_form: Craft damage indicator or visual degradation
danger_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability increasing; continue/game over risk rising

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible icons (weapon upgrades, bombs) floating in play area
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary tactical advantage; reduces near-term danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Wide-area explosion effect covering screen or large sector
safety_signals.meaning: Projectile nullification + damage window; brief safety window created

safety_signals.visual_form: Enhanced projectile effect when two Fighting Thunder craft fire in proximity
safety_signals.meaning: Coordinated safety mechanic; players can amplify defensive output

safety_signals.visual_form: Sprite release additional items after player craft is destroyed
safety_signals.meaning: Mitigation of death penalty; recover offensive capability despite loss

safety_signals.visual_form: Transition screen or visual confirmation of boss defeat
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary respite before difficulty escalation; checkpoint reached

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Crystal-based geometric enemy design; mechanical insectoid or angular forms
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple unit types (fighter-class, heavy units, swarm drones); boss variants with Crystal-integrated architecture
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Alien landscapes, crystalline structures in background, corrupted Earth architecture hijacked by invaders

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Fighting Thunder aircraft design; player-controlled craft embodies human defensive technology
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Human military installations, Earth-based terrain phases, technology derived from Crystal reverse-engineering

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Alien architecture dominating environment; Earth locations corrupted or under occupation; crystalline particle effects and otherworldly color palettes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol and attack in organized waves; boss encounters represent defended positions; stage difficulty escalates as invasion deepens

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: VCD Fighting Thunder is sole visible human defense platform; no friendly units present; player-only presence on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must solo-clear or cooperatively clear stages; no allied support; continuation mechanic emphasizes human persistence despite losses

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss destruction; transition to next stage environment; visual reset and escalation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Difficulty ramp; enemy types upgraded; stage cycle returns to Stage 1 at increased threat level after Stage 8 completion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual wear on player craft or life counter depletion; screen may show continue prompt
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game state shifts to game-over or continue state; world effectively pauses pending player resource investment (credit insertion in arcade)

environmental_storytelling.technique: Stage progression through contaminated Earth locations
environmental_storytelling.example: Stages 1-8 cycle through different biomes (urban, industrial, natural, crystalline zones) showing invasion's global scope and Crystal occupation of multiple territories
Earth structures gradually replaced or merged with Crystal geometric forms; architectural decay visible in background layers indicating long occupation or rapid takeover

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy unit diversity reflecting faction control
environmental_storytelling.example: Different Crystal unit types in each stage imply adapted invasion force; heavy units in defended zones, swarms in dense areas—suggests tactical alien deployment
Fighting Thunder craft visual design blends human military silhouette with Crystal-derived technology; visual language conveys desperate human adaptation using captured alien science

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Regular attack patterns; coordinated fire; evasion of player projectiles; formation-based movement indicating organized invasion tactics
Multi-phase attack patterns; screen-dominating presence; escalating projectile density; retreat/advance movement indicating defensive territory protection
Full maneuverability; weapon systems optimal; responsive control indicating operational capability
Transforms into damaging projectiles; continues threat to enemies; visual indicator that defeat carries momentum cost to invaders
Float toward bottom of screen; recoverable by player; visual priority draw indicating tactical value and reward signal

notes: Raiden Trad (SNES 1992) is a vertically-scrolling shoot 'em up where visual communication centers on alien invasion occupation versus human last-stand defense. The game uses enemy density, projectile patterns, and environmental corruption as primary danger signals. Faction identity is conveyed through Crystal vs. Human technological aesthetics rather than explicit color schemes or heraldry. World state (invasion progress, occupation depth, human tactical position) manifests through stage environment transitions and difficulty escalation. No overt political faction representation—conflict is framed as species-level invasion rather than geopolitical struggle. Safety signals rely on collectible power-ups and cooperative mechanics. Behavioral tells come primarily from enemy attack patterns and movement formations indicating tactical organization of the invading force.
sources_used: wikipedia - Raiden (video game) - Plot, Gameplay, Development sections
